intended_use: Videa Dental Assist is a computer-assisted detection (CADe) device that analyzes intraoral radiographs to identify and localize the following features. Videa Dental Assist is indicated for the review
intended_use_full: Videa Dental Assist is a computer-assisted detection (CADe) device that analyzes intraoral radiographs to identify and localize the following features. Videa Dental Assist is indicated for the review of bitewing, periapical, and panoramic radiographs acquired from patients aged 3 years or older.
target_condition: Multiple dental pathologies and anatomical features including caries, periodontal disease, endodontic lesions, and dental anomalies
ground_truth: US licensed dentists labeled the clinical data and a US licensed dentist adjudicated those labels to establish a reference standard; bench study data ground-truthed by three US board-certified dentists
other_metrics: Bench study: sensitivity and specificity for all indications (specific values not provided in summary); Clinical study: AFROC Figure of Merit (AFROC FOM) improvement ranging from 0.024 (4.3% improvement for caries, p=0.0085) to 0.236 (36.8% improvement for dens invaginatus, p=1.9e-9) across suspect dental finding indications; False positive fraction rate, non-lesion fraction, and positive predictive value also assessed but specific values not provided
